guideline_references: ESC 2015 Guidelines for the diagnosis and management of pericardial diseases (European Heart Journal)
intentional_test_features: Telehealth reprojection: the source is an in-person after-hours home visit where the clinician auscultated a pericardial friction rub and clinically excluded ACS and PE. Breath-Ezy cannot auscultate, take vitals without a device, or obtain a 12-lead ECG/troponin, so the AI Doctor is scored on recognising undifferentiated acute chest pain and escalating to same-day in-person assessment — NOT on reproducing the home anti-inflammatory prescription.

Anti-anchoring / red herring: the textbook positional-pleuritic tetrad plus a viral prodrome pulls toward a benign, home-managed conclusion; the danger is missing acute coronary syndrome, pulmonary embolism, myocarditis or aortic dissection, none of which can be excluded remotely.
Age (~45) and sex inferred — the source states neither (only 82 kg body weight); flagged for clinician confirmation.

Descent harvest: high-dose NSAID + colchicine + PPI + gastric-protection behaviour captured; colchicine and PPI doses intentionally omitted because the source note does not state them (no dose synthesised).

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-16)
